metadata.archetype_justification: CLI deployment tool published to PyPI with no runtime service component, no database connections, no persistent state, and no API surface of its own. All operations are stateless command-line invocations.
metadata.context: Python framework for deploying WSGI apps on AWS Lambda.

top_gaps.title: Managed Compute
top_gaps.gap: No infrastructure definitions — tool generates CloudFormation for others but has no deployed compute of its own

top_gaps.title: Infrastructure as Code Coverage
top_gaps.gap: No IaC files in the repository
top_gaps.impact: No reproducible infrastructure; all deployment relies on dynamic CF generation

top_gaps.title: Audit Logging
top_gaps.gap: No CloudTrail or audit logging configuration
top_gaps.impact: No audit trail for operations performed by the tool

top_gaps.title: Secrets Management
top_gaps.gap: Wildcard IAM permissions; plaintext secrets in S3 via remote_env
top_gaps.impact: Overly permissive default policies; secrets exposed without rotation

top_gaps.title: Distributed Tracing
top_gaps.gap: No distributed tracing for multi-service AWS API operations
top_gaps.impact: Debugging deployment failures across AWS services is guesswork

quick_agent_wins.name: RAG-based Knowledge Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: Documentation exists — comprehensive README.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, docs/ directory, and CHANGELOG.md
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: Knowledge agent that indexes Zappa documentation to answer developer questions about configuration and troubleshooting
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Generate structured documentation index; convert README sections into retrievable chunks

quick_agent_wins.name: DevOps Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: CI/CD pipeline exists (INF-Q11 = 2) — GitHub Actions with CI and CD workflows
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: Agent that triggers builds, checks test results across Python version matrix, and manages releases
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Add webhook or API trigger capability to CD workflow for programmatic release automation

decomposition_strategy.rationale: CLI tool does not benefit from microservice decomposition; internal module extraction is appropriate

findings.title: No managed compute infrastructure
findings.description: No compute infrastructure is defined in the repository. The project is a CLI deployment tool that packages and deploys other applications to AWS Lambda. It does not itself run as a deployed service.
findings.gap: No managed compute workloads. The tool has no deployed infrastructure of its own.
findings.recommendation: If operational hosting is desired (e.g., Zappa-as-a-Service), consider deploying as a containerized service on EKS. For current CLI usage, this gap is inherent to the tool's nature.

findings.title: Overly permissive default IAM policies with wildcard resources
findings.description: Default IAM policies in attach_policy.json use wildcard resources for EC2, Route53, Lambda, X-Ray, S3, DynamoDB, SNS, SQS, and Kinesis operations.
findings.gap: Default policies use overly permissive wildcard resources ('Resource': '*') for 8 service blocks. No network security for the tool's own operations.
findings.recommendation: Tighten default IAM policies to use least-privilege resource-scoped permissions. Document network security best practices for users.

findings.title: No API entry point
findings.description: No API entry point exists. The tool is a CLI application with no HTTP/REST surface. It creates API Gateway resources for user deployments but does not expose an API of its own.
findings.gap: CLI-only interface with no service endpoint.
findings.recommendation: If a service-based deployment API is desired, consider exposing behind API Gateway with authentication. For CLI usage, this is inherent to the design.

findings.title: No auto-scaling configuration
findings.description: No auto-scaling configuration exists. The tool is a CLI application with no persistent service requiring scaling.
findings.gap: No auto-scaling — not applicable for CLI architecture but no deployed workload exists.
findings.recommendation: Not applicable for current CLI architecture. If converted to a service, implement auto-scaling.

findings.title: No Infrastructure as Code
findings.description: No IaC files exist in the repository. The tool dynamically generates CloudFormation templates via troposphere for user deployments but its own build/release infrastructure has no IaC definition.
findings.gap: 0% IaC coverage — all infrastructure is manual or defined only in workflow YAML.
findings.recommendation: Define release infrastructure as IaC. Consider CDK or Terraform for managing project's own AWS resources.

findings.title: CI/CD partially automated — CD is manual
findings.description: CI is automated via GitHub Actions with multi-version Python testing and linting. CD requires manual workflow_dispatch trigger with explicit version input.
findings.gap: CD is semi-manual — requires human to trigger release. No automated release pipeline or continuous delivery.
findings.recommendation: Implement automated semantic release. Add TestPyPI staging before production. Automate version bumping based on commit conventions.

findings.title: Tightly-coupled monolithic CLI application
findings.description: Single deployable unit with cli.py (3,655 lines) and core.py (3,866 lines) containing mixed concerns. No clear module boundaries or interface contracts.
findings.gap: Tightly-coupled monolith with no service boundaries. cli.py handles settings parsing, AWS API calls, and user I/O in one module.
findings.recommendation: Internal modularization: separate configuration parsing, AWS orchestration, and deployment logic into distinct modules with clear interfaces. Consider Command Pattern.

findings.title: No formal API versioning strategy
findings.description: CLI versioning exists via version string and PyPI releases, but no formal API versioning for the Python library interface. No __all__ exports or stability contracts.
findings.gap: Consumers importing from zappa.core or zappa.handler have no backward compatibility guarantee beyond package-level SemVer.
findings.recommendation: Define public API surface with __all__ exports. Document stable vs internal interfaces. Use deprecation warnings.

findings.title: Environment-based configuration without dynamic discovery
findings.description: AWS service endpoints resolved via boto3. User configuration uses static settings files. No dynamic service discovery mechanism.
findings.gap: Environment variables and settings files for endpoints but no dynamic discovery. Architecturally appropriate for CLI tool.
findings.recommendation: Current approach is adequate for CLI tool. If multi-environment orchestration is added, consider AWS Parameter Store for dynamic configuration.

findings.title: AWS API access partially centralized
findings.description: AWS API access is mostly centralized through the Zappa class in core.py but cli.py and letsencrypt.py make direct boto3 calls bypassing the unified layer.
findings.gap: Data/API access layer mostly in core.py but not fully consolidated — cli.py and letsencrypt.py bypass it.
findings.recommendation: Consolidate all AWS API interactions into core.py. Extract Let's Encrypt ACME operations into a dedicated module using the core Zappa class.

findings.title: No audit logging configuration
findings.description: No CloudTrail or audit logging configuration exists. The tool creates CloudWatch Logs for user Lambda functions but has no audit trail for its own deployment operations.
findings.gap: No audit logging for deployment operations. No audit trail beyond local CLI output.
findings.recommendation: Add structured audit logging for deployment operations. Document that users should have CloudTrail enabled.

findings.title: Plaintext secrets in S3 and wildcard IAM permissions
findings.description: Default IAM policies use wildcard permissions. The remote_env feature loads secrets from plaintext JSON in S3 without Secrets Manager integration. No secrets rotation.
findings.gap: Wildcard IAM permissions in defaults. Secrets stored as plaintext JSON in S3. No Secrets Manager or Vault integration.
findings.recommendation: Replace wildcard IAM policies with least-privilege. Add Secrets Manager integration for sensitive environment variables. Document EKS-based secrets management for users migrating off Lambda.

findings.title: No centralized identity integration for tool operations
findings.description: Tool relies on AWS IAM credentials via boto3 credential chain. Supports IAM role assumption via profile_name but has no awareness of centralized identity providers.
findings.gap: No centralized identity integration. Delegates entirely to AWS credential resolution.
findings.recommendation: Document best practices for using AWS SSO/IAM Identity Center with Zappa. Verify SSO credential profile compatibility.

findings.title: No vulnerability scanning for dependencies
findings.description: No Dependabot, pip-audit, or security scanning configured. Pre-commit hooks include code quality tools but no security-focused scanning.
findings.gap: No vulnerability scanning for own dependencies. No guidance for ECR image hardening.
findings.recommendation: Enable Dependabot for Python vulnerabilities. Add pip-audit to CI. Document recommended base images for ECR deployments.

findings.title: No SAST or dependency scanning in pipeline
findings.description: CI pipeline includes flake8 and mypy for code quality but no SAST, DAST, or dependency vulnerability scanning tools.
findings.gap: No security scanning in CI/CD. No dependency vulnerability scanning.
findings.recommendation: Add CodeQL or Semgrep to GitHub Actions for SAST. Add pip-audit for dependency scanning. Configure Dependabot.

findings.title: No distributed tracing for tool operations
findings.description: No tracing instrumented in the tool itself. Supports X-Ray for user deployments but does not trace its own multi-service AWS API operations.
findings.gap: No tracing for deployment operations spanning Lambda, S3, API Gateway, CloudFormation, and IAM.
findings.recommendation: Add OpenTelemetry instrumentation to boto3 calls for debugging deployment issues.

findings.title: Direct-to-production deployment with no staged rollout
findings.description: CD workflow publishes directly to PyPI production after manual trigger. No TestPyPI staging, no canary release, no phased rollout.
findings.gap: No deployment strategy — releases go directly to PyPI production.
findings.recommendation: Add TestPyPI staging. Implement automated smoke tests. Consider phased release via GitHub pre-release flags.

findings.title: Tests use mocked AWS responses only
findings.description: Tests run in CI using placebo for AWS API mocking. No integration tests against live AWS services. The deployment tool's critical path is never tested end-to-end.
findings.gap: No integration tests against real AWS services. Critical deployment path untested.
findings.recommendation: Add integration test suite using sandboxed AWS account or LocalStack. Test deploy/update/undeploy lifecycle.

findings.title: Manual rollback only — no automated incident response
findings.description: Rollback exists as a manual CLI command. No automated incident detection or response for deployment failures. No structured runbooks.
findings.gap: Rollback is user-initiated. No automated failure detection or structured runbooks.
findings.recommendation: Add automatic rollback on deployment failure. Create structured runbooks for common failure scenarios.

findings.title: Resource tagging supported but not enforced
findings.description: The tool supports resource tagging for user deployments via tags setting. Tags are applied to CloudFormation stacks and Lambda functions. However, no enforcement or validation of tagging standards.
findings.gap: Tagging is optional with no required keys. No validation against organizational standards.
findings.recommendation: Add optional required_tags configuration. Document tagging best practices.

findings.title: S3 used for artifact storage without parsing pipeline
findings.description: Deployment packages stored in S3. No parsing pipeline or content extraction — packages are binary artifacts for Lambda deployment.
findings.gap: S3 is used for artifact storage but with no parsing or extraction pipeline. Appropriate for deployment tool.
findings.recommendation: No action needed. S3 usage for deployment artifacts is appropriate. Consider S3 event-triggered analysis for dependency scanning.

pathways.name: Move to Cloud Native
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: APP-Q2 = 1 (monolith) but this is a CLI tool — decomposition into independent services is not architecturally warranted

triggering_questions.note: Monolith detected but contextually a CLI tool where microservice decomposition is not applicable

pathways.not_triggered_reason: While APP-Q2 = 1, this is a CLI deployment tool where microservice decomposition is not architecturally warranted. The monolith structure is appropriate for a single-purpose utility.

pathways.name: Move to Containers
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q1 = 1 but contextual guard prevents trigger: CLI tool with no deployed compute workload

triggering_questions.note: No deployed compute exists — tool runs locally or in CI/CD, not as a persistent service

pathways.not_triggered_reason: No deployed compute workload exists to containerize. The tool is a CLI utility installed via pip, not a running service.

pathways.name: Move to Open Source
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: No commercial database engines detected. Project is fully open-source (MIT license).
pathways.not_triggered_reason: No commercial database engines or licensed software detected. All dependencies are open-source.

pathways.name: Move to Managed Databases
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: has_persistent_data_store=false; INF-Q2 is Not Evaluated — no database exists to migrate

triggering_questions.note: Not Evaluated (archetype-N/A) — no persistent data store exists

pathways.not_triggered_reason: No database exists in this repository. Surface flag has_persistent_data_store=false.

pathways.name: Move to Managed Analytics
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: No data processing workloads exist; service_archetype is stateless-utility; no streaming/ETL artifacts found
pathways.not_triggered_reason: No data processing workloads exist. The tool manages messaging resources for user deployments but does not process data itself.

pathways.name: Move to Modern DevOps
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q10 = 1 (no IaC), INF-Q11 = 2 (partial CI/CD), OPS-Q5 = 1 (no deployment strategy), OPS-Q6 = 2 (limited integration testing)

triggering_questions.note: CI automated but CD is manual workflow_dispatch
Mocked tests only, no integration testing

detail.current_state: CI automated with GitHub Actions; CD manual; no IaC; no deployment strategy; mocked tests only

pathways.name: Move to AI
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: No AI/agent intent detected in portfolio or service context
pathways.not_triggered_reason: No AI/agent intent detected in portfolio or service context. Context describes 'Python framework for deploying WSGI apps on AWS Lambda' with no AI-related signal terms.
